info.title: Porting
info.description:
Bandwidth's Porting API

This API provides endpoints to manage port-in orders, including retrieving and updating activation status for telephone numbers being ported into the Bandwidth network.

get.description: Retrieves the CSR orders for the given account ID. Please visit Guides and Tutorials
Retrieves the CSR order for the given account ID by order ID.

Retrieve the status (activated or not activated) of TNs associated with the customer activated (triggered) order.

At this time all phone numbers associated with a PON will be activated at the same time.

put.description:
Sets the activation time for the port-in order.

This API call is used to set the Activation time of the customer activated (triggered) port.

  • If the time is in the past all of the TNs in the port-in request will be activated 'immediately'.
  • For automated on-net port-ins, if the time is within three days after the approved FoC date, the auto-activation time for the port will be set to that time.
  • For automated off-net port-ins, if the date matches the actual FOC date and the time is between 6:00 AM ET and 10:00 PM ET, the auto-activation time for the port will be set to that time.

get.description: Retrieve information about one or more Telephone Numbers (TNs) with port out passcode.

post.description: Retrieve information about one or more Telephone Numbers (TNs), where the TNs are specified in the POST body.

csrListResponseXmlExample.summary: XML example for listing CSR orders
csrListResponseXmlExample.value:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<CsrResponses>
    <TotalCount>3</TotalCount>
    <CsrResponse>
        <CustomerOrderId>someValue</CustomerOrderId>
        <LastModifiedBy>systemUser</LastModifiedBy>
        <OrderCreateDate>2019-10-25T09:51:17Z</OrderCreateDate>
        <AccountId>1</AccountId>
        <OrderId>a2f55ebe-782a-4242-983b-6ae6cfa97833</OrderId>
        <LastModifiedDate>2019-10-25T12:51:22.139Z</LastModifiedDate>
        <Status>COMPLETE</Status>
        <AccountNumber>987654321</AccountNumber>
        <AccountTelephoneNumber>+19196194444</AccountTelephoneNumber>
        <EndUserName>bandwidthGuy</EndUserName>
        <AuthorizingUserName>importantAuthGuy</AuthorizingUserName>
        <CustomerCode>123</CustomerCode>
        <EndUserPIN>12345</EndUserPIN>
        <AddressLine1>900 Main Campus Dr</AddressLine1>
        <City>Raleigh</City>
        <State>NC</State>
        <ZIPCode>27612</ZIPCode>
        <TypeOfService>residential</TypeOfService>
    </CsrResponse>
</CsrResponses>

value.description: On demand port-in activation not yet allowed.

example.value:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ActivationStatusResponse>
    <ResponseStatus>
        <ErrorCode>7342</ErrorCode>
        <Description>On demand port-in activation not yet allowed.</Description>
    </ResponseStatus>
</ActivationStatusResponse>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ActivationStatusResponse>
    <ResponseStatus>
        <ErrorCode>7348</ErrorCode>
        <Description>The order is already complete</Description>
    </ResponseStatus>
</ActivationStatusResponse>

Basic.description:
Basic authentication is a simple authentication scheme built into the HTTP protocol. To use it, send your HTTP requests with an Authorization header that contains the word Basic followed by a space and a base64-encoded string `username:password`.
Example: `Authorization: Basic dXNlcm5hbWU6cGFzc3dvcmQ=`

Bearer.description:
Bearer authentication (also called token authentication) is an HTTP authentication scheme that involves security 
tokens called bearer tokens. The name "Bearer authentication" can be understood as "give access to the bearer
of this token." The client must send this token in the `Authorization` header when making requests to
protected resources.

- Example: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

Where `<token>` should be replaced with the token string without angle brackets.

For more information, see the following guide: [Credentials](https://dev.bandwidth.com/docs/account/credentials/management)

tnPortoutPasscodesPageQueryParam.name: page
tnPortoutPasscodesPageQueryParam.description: An ID of the first element in a page. This value will indicate the first value, not the count, of the initial entry in the page being requested. Note in the example that the page is the TN that begins the page. The initial page is tagged with the ID `1`.

tnPortoutPasscodesSizeQueryParam.name: size
tnPortoutPasscodesSizeQueryParam.description: The number of items to include in a page.

modifiedDateFromQueryParam.name: modifiedDateFrom
modifiedDateFromQueryParam.description: The earliest date that the order was last modified.

modifiedDateToQueryParam.name: modifiedDateTo
modifiedDateToQueryParam.description: The latest date that the order was last modified. If no modifiedDateTo is specified when a modifiedDateFrom is specified then 'now' will be presumed to be the modifiedDateTo.

pageQueryParam.name: page
pageQueryParam.description: The CSR order id corresponding to the page that you want to start from. "1" is used as a convention for the first CSR order for the account. Defaults to 1, if not provided.

sizeQueryParam.name: size
sizeQueryParam.description: The maximum number of results to retrieve.

statusQueryParam.name: status
statusQueryParam.description: Displays the CSR orders with a given order status.

csrRequestJson.description: JSON request body for creating or updating a CSR order.

customerOrderId.description: Optional customer-defined reference ID for this order.

workingOrBillingTelephoneNumber.description: Working or Billing telephone number in E.164 format.

accountNumber.description: The main account number on the bill.

accountTelephoneNumber.description: The account telephone number assigned by the current service provider.

endUserName.description: The name of the end user associated with the telephone number.

authorizingUserName.description: The name of the end user who signed the authorization.

customerCode.description: The customer code associated with the service provider.

endUserPin.description: The end user personal identification number (PIN).

endUserPassword.description: The end user password to access the CSR, if applicable.

addressLine1.description: Address line 1 portion of the service address.

city.description: City where the telephone number is being serviced.

zipCode.description: ZIP code or postal code where the telephone number is being serviced.

typeOfService.description: The type of service identifying the end user account.

workingTelephoneNumber.description: Working telephone number in E.164 format.

billingTelephoneNumber.description: Billing telephone number in E.164 format.

customerName.description: The subscriber name associated with the telephone number.

authorizingPersonName.description: The name of the person authorizing the CSR request.

workingTelephoneNumbersOnAccount.description: List of all telephone numbers on the account.

csrServiceAddress.description: The service address for the CSR.

csrOrderStatus.description: The current status of the CSR order.

csrOrderResponseJson.description: JSON response for a created CSR order.

orderId.description: The Bandwidth-assigned identifier for this CSR order.

csrGetResponseJson.description: JSON representation of a single CSR order.

csrResponsesJson.description: JSON representation of a list of CSR orders for an account.

AutoActivationDate.description: The date when auto activation is requested. It can be a date/time in the past, if immediate activation is desired.

ActivatedTelephoneNumbersList.description: This is the list of TNs that have been activated for the port-in order.

NotYetActivatedTelephoneNumbersList.description: This is the list of TNs that have not yet been activated for the port-in order.

activatedPhoneNumbers.description: List of phone numbers that have been activated for the port-in order.

notYetActivatedPhoneNumbers.description: List of phone numbers that have not yet been activated for the port-in order.

autoActivationDate.description: The date when auto activation is requested. RFC 3339 format.

phoneNumbers.description: List of phone numbers with their associated port-out passcodes.

fullNumber.description: Phone number in E.164 format starting with '+'. Example: +15551234567

passcode.description: Port-out passcode for the phone number.

phoneNumbers.description: List of phone numbers in E.164 format.

tags.name: Porting
tags.description: Manage port-in orders and their activation status.
